title: ProcessOwnershipLedger START + STOP registration on every sandbox spawn (perf-mitigated batched/async/capped path)
goal: Every model process and validation process spawned via SandboxAdapter MUST register START + STOP rows in the ProcessOwnershipLedger Postgres table per spec §5.7.2 / AC-LOCAL-MODEL-BOXING. This MT lands the cross-cutting wiring that intercepts every spawn/kill on every SandboxAdapter, batches writes per the perf-mitigated path (R-11 mitigation: batched async writes + per-process metadata cap + FR-EVT-LEDGER-OVERFLOW degraded mode per spec §5.7.3), and exposes the live process registry to the Diagnostics Panel.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/{mod.rs, writer.rs, batcher.rs, overflow.rs, schema.rs} (Postgres table is added in cluster A.4 MT — coordinate; if not yet present, this MT lands the schema migration too at src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/<ts>_kernel_process_lifecycle.sql); SandboxAdapter wrapper decorator src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/ledger_decorator.rs that wraps any Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter> and emits START/STOP on spawn/kill/status transitions; bootstrap (MT-048) updated to wrap each adapter in the ledger decorator before registering. Tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/process_ledger_writer_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: SandboxAdapter trait + bootstrap merged. If cluster A.4 lands the kernel_process_lifecycle Postgres schema independently, defer schema.sql here to a no-op reference; otherwise own the migration.
implementation_notes: Schema (only land if cluster A.4 has not): `CREATE TABLE kernel_process_lifecycle ( process_id UUID PRIMARY KEY (v7), adapter_id TEXT NOT NULL, sandbox_internal_id TEXT NOT NULL, parent_process_id UUID, engine_kind TEXT NOT NULL, role_id TEXT, wp_id TEXT, mt_id TEXT, spawned_at_utc TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, stopped_at_utc TIMESTAMPTZ, exit_code INT, stop_reason TEXT, sandbox_capabilities_snapshot JSONB NOT NULL, metadata_jsonb JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb )` plus index on (adapter_id, spawned_at_utc) and (wp_id, spawned_at_utc). Per CX-503R: NO SQLite — Postgres only. Per R-11 mitigations: writes go through a `LedgerBatcher` (tokio task with mpsc channel; flushes every 100 events OR every 250ms whichever first; per-process metadata cap = 16KB JSONB blob — overflow triggers FR-EVT-LEDGER-OVERFLOW row to EventLedger and the metadata field gets `{ "capped": true, "original_bytes": N }`). LedgerDecorator wraps any `Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>` and on spawn() emits a START row (calling LedgerBatcher::record_start asynchronously — must not block spawn latency), on kill() and on observed status transitions to Exited/Killed emits a STOP row. Decorator is transparent to callers (implements SandboxAdapter itself, delegating to inner). MT-048 bootstrap is updated: each constructed adapter is wrapped via `let adapter = Arc::new(LedgerDecorator::new(inner_adapter, ledger_batcher.clone())) as Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>` before registry.register(). Failure-mode discipline: if Postgres is unreachable, LedgerBatcher MUST NOT block spawn — it queues into an in-memory ring buffer (cap 10_000 events) and emits FR-EVT-LEDGER-OVERFLOW when the ring overflows; on Postgres recovery the buffered events flush in spawn order. Tests: (a) wrapping a NoopAdapter and calling spawn() enqueues exactly one START event; (b) calling kill() enqueues exactly one STOP event with the correct stop_reason; (c) overflow test: enqueue 10_001 events with Postgres unreachable and verify the 10_001st event triggers FR-EVT-LEDGER-OVERFLOW; (d) per-process metadata cap test: a 20KB metadata blob is truncated to 16KB and the capped marker is set; (e) replay-equivalence: two test runs with identical spawn/kill sequences produce identical kernel_process_lifecycle row sets (HBR-INT-001).
risk_if_missed: Without ledger registration the AC-LOCAL-MODEL-BOXING requirement ('every model process Handshake spawns MUST be registered with START + STOP rows') fails the §5.6 HBR gate; spawn-latency regression under swarm concurrency (R-11) is the most likely defect mode.
validator_focus: (a) LedgerDecorator is the ONLY path that writes to kernel_process_lifecycle (no direct INSERT statements anywhere else); (b) spawn-latency overhead from decorator wrapping is <5ms p95 under unit-test load; (c) overflow degraded-mode works (Postgres-down test); (d) UUID v7 for process_id; (e) HBR-INT-001 replay equivalence holds.

minimum_controls: Repo-wide grep test asserts no `INSERT INTO kernel_process_lifecycle` outside src/backend/handshake_core/src/process_ledger/** (LedgerDecorator is the sole write path).
Postgres-unreachable overflow test prevents silent loss of spawn events on temporary DB outage (R-11 mitigation).
Spawn-latency micro-benchmark (criterion crate or simple Instant timing) keeps decorator overhead below 5ms p95 under HBR-SWARM-001 concurrent load.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: ProcessOwnershipLedger (kernel_process_lifecycle table) + LedgerBatcher + LedgerDecorator + cap_metadata_jsonb + ring-buffer overflow path with FR_EVT_LEDGER_OVERFLOW are all implemented; UUID v7 used; START/STOP record_once guards prevent duplicate STOP rows; Postgres migration SQL is shipped; tests cover ring overflow, metadata cap, batching defaults.
